posts.text:
Told @krystalball  Trump struck Kharg island, but refrained from hitting the oil facilities out of fear of what Iran's retaliation would do to gas prices. Tehran will likely interpret this as a sign that reality is sinking in - Trump finally understands that he can't escalate his way out of this debacle.

piped.video/watch?v=NNRUPcGE…

MUST read!

"It is certainly true that China is not willing to play the same role for Iran that the United States has long assumed for its own partners. But that does not mean that China is feckless, nor does it mean that its ties with Iran are insincere. It means, above all, that too many observers still measure every rising power against a U.S. template."

foreignpolicy.com/2026/03/06…
The US embassy urges ALL Americans to leave Iraq immediately...

Meloni’s “balancing act” with Trump “finally broke down with the war in Iran,” said Ferruccio De Bortoli, a former editor in chief of an Italian newspaper. “Her great concern today is of course losing support because of a war that isn’t her own.” washingtonpost.com/world/202…

This story has not gotten enough attention. 

A senior French military official was killed in Iraqi Kurdistan by an Iranian drone two days ago. No details have been provided by the French as to why he was there in the first place, except saying it was "defensive". 

According to the Iranians, Iraqi Kurdish authorities tipped off Tehran that the French were in Kurdistan seeking to arm and help Iranian Kurdish militias to attack Iran.
